name: Czech Republic

authority.name: Digitální a informační agentura (DIA) — Digital and Information Agency, Czech eIDAS supervisory body and national Trusted List scheme operator
authority.operator: Accredited QTSPs under eIDAS (První certifikační autorita, a.s. (I.CA), Česká pošta, s.p. / PostSignum, Software602 a.s. / ACAeID, eIdentity a.s., and the Komerční banka root, among others)

governingLaw: Reglamento eIDAS (UE) 910/2014 · Zákon č. 297/2016 Sb., o službách vytvářejících důvěru pro elektronické transakce

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the Czech Trusted List (TSL_CZ.xtsl, DIA)

relatedLinks.label: EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: The Czech Republic's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (První certifikační autorita, a.s. (I.CA), Česká pošta, s.p. / PostSignum, Software602 a.s. / ACAeID, eIdentity a.s., and the Komerční banka root, among others) supervised by the Digitální a informační agentura (DIA), the Digital and Information Agency acting as the Czech eIDAS supervisory body, and published in the Czech national eIDAS Trusted List (TSL_CZ.xtsl, ETSI TS 119 612). The 34 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Czech TSL's signature (signer: G=Filip, SN=Bílek, CN=Filip Bílek, O=Digitální a informační agentura, 2.5.4.97=NTRCZ-17651921, C=CZ; cert SHA-256 553819860388eb5dc4836bf16897a58a0964cd04d1acddfc4b2e36d0908e10ae) against the identity the LOTL declares for the Czech Republic, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Key algorithms across the set are mixed: 12 CAs use RSA-2048, 10 use RSA-4096, 6 use RSA-3072, 5 use EC P-521, and 1 uses EC P-384. Fourteen certificates in the set have passed their notAfter and are retained as archived-but-granted entries; they surface under the Expired status filter.
